title: Race-lap group ranking model stacked with classifier OOF scores
summary: ROC AUC is a ranking metric, and official XGBoost/CatBoost ranking docs support groupwise or pairwise training by query.
rationale: ROC AUC is a ranking metric, and official XGBoost/CatBoost ranking docs support groupwise or pairwise training by query. Here, each race-lap can be treated as a query where the task is to rank which cars are most likely to pit on the next lap; this complements a pointwise classifier rather than replacing it.
implementation_hint: Train an `XGBRanker` or CatBoost ranking model with query/group ID = `(Race, Year, LapNumber)` or `(Race, Year, LapNumber, regime bucket)`, using current engineered features plus traffic/hazard features. Generate strict OOF rank scores, rank-normalize them, and blend with the pointwise model via a tiny fold-safe stacker.
expected_effect: Can improve ordering among simultaneously exposed cars and add diversity for ensemble gain even if standalone AUC is only similar.
risk: If many laps have zero or one positives, ranking objectives can be unstable; restrict to informative groups or weight groups by number of effective pairs.
